what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG pause icon made from two vertical rounded rectangles using currentColor for styling.
observations: The root svg uses viewBox="0 0 24 24" with explicit width and height of 24.
The root sets fill="none", stroke="currentColor", stroke-width="1.5", stroke-linecap="round", and stroke-linejoin="round".
The icon is drawn with two rect elements positioned at x="7" and x="13.5".
Both rectangles are 3.5 units wide, 14 units tall, start at y="5", and use rx="0.5".
Both bars set fill="currentColor" and stroke-linejoin="round".
standouts: The pause symbol is constructed from simple filled geometric bars.
The icon color is inherited from currentColor.
The SVG is compact and self-contained.
conventions: Uses currentColor so the icon can follow surrounding text or CSS color.
Uses rounded joins and small corner radii for softened geometry.
Combines a no-fill root with child shapes that explicitly fill themselves.
concerns: The rectangles inherit the parent stroke while also using fill="currentColor", so the bars render as filled shapes with stroked edges.
notes: A compact currentColor SVG pause icon composed of two rounded vertical bars.
questions: Should the pause bars intentionally inherit a stroke as well as a fill?
purpose_vs_actual: The path and filename indicate a pause icon, and the file draws a pause symbol; the stated purpose matches the actual content.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a reusable pause control glyph that can adapt to the current text or UI color.
contribution: Contributes a self-contained, themeable pause icon asset built from simple SVG rectangles for use in a fused design system.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 currentColor SVG pause icon composed of two filled rounded vertical rectangles.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for representing a pause action as a reusable design-system icon asset.
